originalTitle: South African president says he will not step down after impeachment call
title: Our President from South Africa is Staying! Big News for Everyone!
content:
Hello there, amazing friends! It's your friendly reporter from Africa, here with some super important and exciting news! Grab a comfy seat, because we're going to talk about a very special person in a beautiful, sunny country.

Meet Our President from South Africa!

Imagine a big, colourful country right at the bottom of Africa. It's a place full of amazing animals like lions, elephants, and giraffes, and bright, happy people who speak many different languages. This wonderful place is called South Africa! Just like your school has a principal who helps make everything run smoothly, a whole country has a leader. This leader is called a President. The President helps make big decisions for everyone in the country, from schools to roads to making sure people are safe and happy. The President of South Africa is a kind man named Mr. Cyril Ramaphosa. He's been the leader for some time, working hard to make South Africa a great place for all its children and families. He has a very important job, thinking about what is best for everyone.

Big News: Our President is Staying!

Now, for the really big news! For a little while, many grown-ups were talking and wondering if President Ramaphosa would leave his important job. It was like when you're waiting for a big announcement, and everyone is guessing what it will be! People were talking a lot, asking many questions, and thinking about what might happen next. This is what grown-ups call 'speculation' – when people guess and talk about something before they know for sure. But guess what happened? On Monday evening, President Ramaphosa stood up and made a very clear announcement to everyone. He said, "I am NOT going to step down!" This means he is staying right where he is, as the leader of South Africa! Isn't that something? He decided to keep his big job and continue working hard for all the people in South Africa. He wants to keep leading and helping his country grow strong and happy.

What Does a President Do?

You might be asking, "What exactly does a President do all day?" Well, it's a very big job that takes a lot of care and smart thinking! A President is like the captain of a very, very big ship – the whole country! They help decide how to make schools better, how to build more homes, and how to create jobs so that grown-ups can earn money for their families. They also meet with leaders from other countries to make friends and work together, which is super important for everyone. It's a job that needs a lot of listening, planning, and making tough decisions. **Did you know?** The President of a country is chosen by the people! Grown-ups vote to pick who they think will be the best leader for everyone. It's a very special way to choose who leads your country!

Why Were There Questions?

Now, you might be wondering why people were talking about President Ramaphosa leaving his job. Sometimes, even the most important leaders face challenges or questions about how things are done. There were some grown-ups who felt that some rules might have been broken, or that things weren't done in the best way. Imagine if someone in charge of your class was questioned about how they organised a special event, and people wondered if they should still be in charge. It was a bit like that, but for the whole country! Some groups of people even said they wanted him to leave his job because of these questions. This is what the big grown-ups call an "impeachment call." It simply means people asking a leader to leave their job because of very serious concerns or if they think the leader has done something wrong. It's a very serious thing, and it doesn't happen often. It shows how important it is for leaders to follow all the rules and be fair to everyone. All these talks made everyone wait to see what President Ramaphosa would decide.

President Ramaphosa's Important Choice

President Ramaphosa listened to all the discussions and thought very, very hard about everything. He knew it was a huge decision, not just for him, but for every single person in South Africa. He thought about what was best for his country, for the children, for the families, and for the future. After much thought, he decided that his heart and his duty told him to stay. He wants to make sure South Africa keeps moving forward, making life better and brighter for everyone. He believes he can still help solve problems and make South Africa a stronger, happier, and more fair place for all its amazing people. **Did you know?** South Africa is famous for its beautiful flowers, especially the Protea, which is their national flower! It's big and colourful, just like the country!

What Happens Now?

So, now that President Ramaphosa has told everyone he will not step down, what happens next? Well, he will continue to be the President of South Africa! He will keep his important job! He will go back to work, leading the country, making decisions, and trying his very best to help everyone. It means that the country will continue to have its leader, which is important for things to stay steady and calm. He will keep working on important plans for schools, for helping grown-ups find jobs, and for making sure everyone is healthy and safe. It's very important for a country to have a strong and steady leader, especially when there are challenges. President Ramaphosa said he is committed to serving the people of South Africa. That means he promises to work for them with all his heart and do his best to build a better future for everyone. So, our friendly President from South Africa is staying to keep leading his beautiful country! **Did you know?** South Africa has 11 official languages! That's a lot of ways to say "hello" and "thank you"! It shows how many different cultures and people live there together!
summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa of South Africa announced he will not leave his job, deciding to continue leading the country after many days of questions and talks about his leadership.
whyMatters: This news is important because having a steady leader helps a country stay calm and keep working on making life better for everyone, especially for children and families in South Africa.

originalTitle: Dozens of Nigerian fishermen feared dead after Chad air strikes on Boko Haram
title: Worried Friends Near Lake Chad
content:
Hello, Friends!

Hi everyone, it's your friendly news reporter for Africa! Today, we have some news to share from a beautiful part of Africa, near a big lake called Lake Chad. Sometimes, sad things happen, but it's important to talk about them and understand what's going on. And remember, there are always people working to help!

About Our Friends, The Fishermen

Near Lake Chad, many wonderful people live and work. A lot of them are called fishermen. Can you guess what fishermen do? That's right! They go out onto the lake in their boats to catch fish. Fish is a very important food for many families, and catching them helps people earn a living to buy other things they need. It's a very important job! Fishermen know the lake very well. They know where the fish like to hide and how to stay safe on the water. They are usually very brave and strong. Their families depend on them to bring home food every day. Did you know? Fish live in water, and there are so many different kinds of fish in the world, some big and some tiny! Fishermen help bring these tasty fish to our plates and to markets all over. It’s a very old and important job in Africa.

Where This Story Happens: Lake Chad

This news comes from a place around Lake Chad. Lake Chad is a very, very big lake in Africa. It touches four different countries! Can you imagine? These countries are called Chad, Nigeria, Niger, and Cameroon. The lake is like a big, blue heart connecting all these places. Many people live all around Lake Chad. They grow food, raise animals, and of course, fish in the lake. It's a very busy and important place for many families to live and find food. Thousands of people rely on Lake Chad every day. Did you know? Lake Chad used to be even bigger many years ago! It has changed over time, but it's still a huge and vital source of water and life for millions of people. It provides water for drinking, farming, and cooking for many communities.

What Happened Recently

Recently, something happened that made people very worried. There were some big planes flying in the sky over parts of Lake Chad. These planes belong to the army of Chad. They were trying to help keep people safe from bad groups. When these planes fly and do their job to stop bad people, it's called an "air strike." It means they are trying to stop trouble from the air. This can be very loud and scary for people on the ground. During these air strikes, some fishermen were out on the lake doing their work, just like they do every day. A leader for the fishermen has said that about 40 people might be missing. Some might have been hurt by the loud noise or movements from the planes, and others might have accidentally fallen into the water while trying to get away quickly because they were scared. It's a very sad thing to think about, and everyone hopes that the missing people will be found safe soon. People are looking for them and praying for them.

Who Are Boko Haram?

Now, you might be wondering why the army planes were flying in the first place. This is because there is a group of people called "Boko Haram." These are not good people. They like to cause trouble and hurt innocent people, even children. They don't want children to go to school, and they try to scare everyone in their communities. Boko Haram has caused a lot of problems in Nigeria and other countries around Lake Chad for a long time. They try to stop people from living peacefully and doing their daily jobs. They are a big worry for many families in this area. Did you know? "Boko Haram" means "Western education is forbidden" in one of the local languages. But we know that learning and going to school are very important and good! Education helps us grow and build a better future.

Why Soldiers Are Helping

Because Boko Haram causes so much trouble, the armies from the countries around Lake Chad, like Chad and Nigeria, work together. Their job is to protect innocent people from these bad groups. They want everyone to be safe and live without fear, especially the children. So, the army planes from Chad were flying to try and stop Boko Haram. They were trying to make the area safe again for families, for children to play, and for people like the fishermen to do their jobs without worrying. It’s like when a big brother or sister protects their younger siblings from bullies, but on a much bigger scale.

Finding Out What Happened and Helping

When something sad like this happens, it's very important to find out exactly what happened. People are working hard to understand everything. They are talking to people who saw what happened, and they are trying to find the missing fishermen. It's a time for everyone to come together and help each other. Families are supporting each other, and leaders are trying to make sure such things don't happen again to innocent people. They want to make sure that people who are just trying to do their daily work are safe and protected.

Hope for Peace and Safety

Even when sad news comes, we must always remember that there is hope. Many good people are working very hard to bring peace and safety to Lake Chad and all of Africa. Soldiers are trying their best to protect, and communities are helping each other in every way they can. They are showing great courage and kindness. We hope that the missing fishermen are found and that everyone can live safely near the beautiful Lake Chad. It's important to be kind and help others, especially when they are going through a difficult time. Let's send our kind thoughts to everyone in the Lake Chad region. We want everyone to be happy, safe, and able to go about their lives peacefully.
summary: Some fishermen are missing near Lake Chad after army planes tried to stop a bad group, making people sad and hoping everyone will be found safe.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone deserves to be safe while doing their jobs, and we must make sure innocent people are protected when armies are trying to stop bad groups like Boko Haram.

originalTitle: Whales could be harmed by diverted ships avoiding Middle East, scientists warn
title: Big Ships, New Paths, and Our Whale Friends!
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hello everyone, from your kid-friendly news reporter right here in beautiful Africa! We have an important story today about our big blue ocean, amazing animals, and giant ships. Imagine a world where huge, huge boats, bigger than many houses, sail across the water. These boats carry lots of things we use every day. They usually follow certain paths. But now, many of them are taking a new, longer path, right around the tip of our amazing continent, Africa! This change can affect some very special friends who live in our ocean: the whales. Let's find out more together!

What Are Big Ships Doing?

Imagine a giant, giant boat. So big, it looks like a floating city! These are called ships. They are not like the small boats you might see at the beach. These ships travel across the very, very big ocean. They go to many countries, far, far away. What do these super big ships carry? Oh, so many things! They carry the toys you play with. They carry the clothes you wear. They carry yummy food for our homes and even cars! Without these ships, it would be hard to get many things we use every day. These ships are always on an adventure, taking long journeys. They have special paths they usually follow on the ocean, like roads for cars. But something has changed recently for many of these ships. They are taking a different path now. Did you know? Some ships are so big they can be as long as four or five football fields put together! That's super long!

Ships Are Taking a Different Road!

So, what does it mean to take a "different road"? It means the ships have "rerouted." Imagine you walk to school every day on one path. But then, one day, there's a big puddle or something blocking your usual way. So, you decide to take a different, maybe longer, path to get to school safely. That's what ships are doing! Instead of their usual way, many ships are now sailing all the way around the bottom of Africa. This special tip of Africa is called the Cape of Good Hope. It's a famous and beautiful place in South Africa, right here on our continent! This new path means the ships are spending more time sailing near our African coasts. It's a much longer journey for them, but they are doing it for a very important reason. Did you know? The Cape of Good Hope got its name because sailors hoped to find a sea route to Asia, which meant more trade and good things for their countries!

Why Ships Are Taking a New Way

Why would ships take a longer, slower journey? It's because of something called "conflict" in a place far away called the Middle East. "Conflict" means that people are not getting along, and sometimes it can be unsafe there. Just like you might avoid a noisy, busy road to stay safe, these ships are avoiding the places where there is conflict. They want to keep all the sailors on board safe. They also want to keep all the important things they are carrying safe too. So, since the year 2023, many big ships have started using this new, safer path around our lovely Cape of Good Hope. It's a smart choice for safety, but it brings up new things we need to think about. Did you know? Before these big ships, people used to travel the oceans in much smaller sailing boats, taking even longer to reach distant lands!

Who Lives in Our Big Blue Ocean? Whales!

Now, let's talk about some of the most wonderful creatures that live in our oceans: whales! Whales are giant, gentle animals that swim and play in the big blue sea. They are amazing to watch, sometimes jumping high out of the water! There are many different kinds of whales. Some are called Humpback whales, and others are Southern Right whales. They are very important for our ocean's health. They have their own special ways of living, just like we do. Whales travel, or "migrate," long distances in the ocean. They go to warm waters to have their babies and then to colder waters to find lots of food. The waters around the Cape of Good Hope are a very special place for them, especially for mothers and their baby whales. Did you know? Whales are mammals, just like us! This means they breathe air, have hair (though not much!), and mothers feed their babies milk. They are not fish!

How Big Ships Can Harm Our Whale Friends

This is where the story gets a little bit sad. When many, many big ships take the same path, especially a new path, it can sometimes be a problem for our whale friends. Imagine you are trying to talk to your friend, but there are lots of very noisy cars going past. It would be hard to hear, right? Well, ships make a lot of noise under the water. This loud noise can make it hard for whales to "talk" to each other. Whales sing and make special sounds to find food and stay together. The extra noise can make it difficult for whale mothers to find their babies, or for whales to find their yummy fish to eat. It can confuse them. Another sad thing is that sometimes, a very, very big ship might accidentally bump into a whale. Because there are now more ships in a place where whales swim, the chance of this happening goes up. This can make whales very sick or hurt them badly, especially the smaller, playful baby whales. Did you know? Whales use a kind of sound called "echolocation" to find their way around and hunt for food in the dark parts of the ocean. Ship noise can mess with their special hearing!

What Smart Scientists Are Saying

Who are scientists? They are very smart people who study animals, plants, and how the world works. They spend a lot of time watching and listening to our ocean and the creatures in it. They are like detectives for nature! These scientists are watching the ships and they are watching our whale friends. They have seen that more ships are now sailing in the waters where whales love to play and have their babies. The scientists are worried. They are saying, "Please be careful!" They want to protect our beautiful whale friends. They are telling everyone that we need to find ways for ships to be safe, and for whales to be safe too. They are warning us so that we can all work together to make sure both the ships and the whales can use the ocean safely and happily. It's very important to listen to what our scientists are telling us. Did you know? Scientists use special underwater microphones called hydrophones to listen to the amazing songs of whales deep in the ocean!

What Can We Do to Help?

Even though we are kids, we can do important things to help! We can learn more about whales and our oceans. The more we know, the more we care! We can tell our friends and family about this story. We can ask grown-ups who work with big ships to be extra careful. Maybe ships can slow down when they are in areas where whales like to be. Slower ships make less noise! Maybe they can use different paths that are far away from where the whales have their babies. Every little bit of kindness and care helps. Our planet is one big home, and we all need to share it kindly. Did you know? When ships travel slower, they not only make less noise but also use less fuel, which is better for the air we breathe too!

Let's Protect Our Ocean Friends!

Our oceans are amazing places, full of wonder and incredible creatures like our whale friends. The Cape of Good Hope, right here in Africa, is a very special part of this big, blue home. We want ships to be able to travel safely and bring us all the important things we need. But we also want our gentle whale friends to be able to swim, play, sing, and have their babies without being harmed. By learning about these changes and thinking about how our actions affect others, we can make a difference. Let's all be good stewards of our planet and protect our beautiful ocean and all its wonderful inhabitants. Thank you for listening!
summary: Giant ships are taking a new, longer path around the tip of Africa to stay safe, but scientists are worried this new route could harm our amazing whale friends who live in those waters.
whyMatters: This matters because big ships are taking a new route around Africa, and more ships mean more noise and more chances to hurt the whales that live and have their babies in those waters. We need to find ways for both ships and whales to be safe so we can protect these amazing ocean animals.

originalTitle: Last passengers leave virus-hit cruise ship as three more test positive
title: Staying Healthy from Tiny Germs!
content:
Jambo, My Friends!

Jambo, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with some important news. We want all of you to be strong and healthy so you can play, learn, and have lots of fun every day. Today, we are talking about something very, very tiny. So tiny you cannot even see it! We call these tiny things 'germs' or 'viruses'. They can sometimes make people feel a little bit sick. We heard some news from far away. Two people, one from America and one from France, were found to have a special kind of tiny germ called 'hantavirus'. They are now getting better, which is good news!

What is Hantavirus?

Don't worry, hantavirus is not something that jumps from person to person like a cough or a sneeze. It's a bit different. This tiny germ comes from little animals we call mice and rats. Yes, those little creatures that sometimes run around! When mice or rats run around, they might leave their pee or poo in places. Sometimes, tiny bits of this can get into the air, like dust. If you breathe in this special dust, it can sometimes make you sick. But remember, it's not from other people! It's from mice and rats. Did you know? Mice are very good at hiding! They can squeeze into tiny holes you might not even notice.

How Can We Stay Safe and Strong?

Staying safe from tiny germs like hantavirus is easy if we know what to do! It's all about keeping things clean and being smart.

Wash, Wash, Wash Your Hands!

This is the super best way to keep germs away! Always wash your hands with soap and clean water. Wash them often! Wash your hands after you play outside, after you use the toilet, and especially before you eat food. Make sure you rub them for a long time, like singing 'Happy Birthday' two times! Did you know? Washing your hands for just 20 seconds helps to wash away almost all the tiny germs!

Keep Your Homes Clean!

We love our homes, and keeping them clean helps us stay healthy. Try to keep your food in places where mice and rats cannot reach it. Put your food in special containers with lids. Clean up any food that spills on the floor quickly. This makes sure mice and rats don't find yummy snacks in your home. Ask your grown-ups to help keep the house tidy. A clean house is a happy and healthy house!

Be Careful Around Wild Animals!

Mice and rats live in the wild, just like many other animals. They are part of nature. But it's best not to touch them. If you see a mouse or a rat, tell a grown-up. Don't try to catch them or play with them. It's better to admire them from far away. Did you know? Some mice can have more than 50 babies in just one year! That's a lot of tiny mice!

Tell a Grown-Up if You Don't Feel Well!

If you ever feel sick, like if you have a tummy ache, or feel very tired, or have a warm head (a fever), always tell your mom, dad, or another grown-up you trust. They are your super-helpers! They will know what to do and can take you to a doctor if you need to go. Doctors and nurses are also super-helpers who can make you feel better.

We Are Strong Together!

Remember, getting sick from hantavirus is very, very rare. Most people are healthy and strong! By following these simple rules – washing your hands, keeping things clean, and being smart around wild animals – you are helping yourself and everyone around you stay healthy. Let's all be super-hygiene heroes and keep our beautiful homes and communities clean and safe! This way, we can all have lots of energy for playing, learning, and making Africa shine brightly! Did you know? Clean water is not just for drinking! It's super important for washing your hands and keeping things clean too! Stay healthy and keep smiling, my wonderful friends!
summary: Two people from far away countries were found to have a tiny germ called hantavirus, which comes from mice and rats, and we can stay safe by washing hands, keeping homes clean, and not touching wild animals.
whyMatters: It's important to know about tiny germs like hantavirus so we can learn how to keep ourselves strong and healthy by washing our hands and keeping our homes clean, which helps us play and learn every day.

content: Two people are being monitored in Atlanta, while 16 others are in Nebraska at the nation's only national quarantine unit.
